summary: index-ulid 3.0.2: bundled payload contains multiple POST sites; behavior unverified
details: The package ships a bundled file at dist/node/payload.js containing multiple HTTP POST call sites (lines 14503, 14532, 14716, 14834, 15716). Pattern matching alone cannot distinguish a benign HTTP client SDK from an exfiltration channel: the same keyword shape arises from documented API clients, telemetry libraries, and attack code. The destination hosts, the data being POSTed, the call-site reachability from install/import time, and whether any lifecycle hook invokes this code path were not established. A package named for ULID generation typically should not require outbound HTTP at all, so the presence of a large bundled payload with multiple POST sites is unusual and warrants human review of dist/node/payload.js to confirm whether the calls go to a documented API, what data they carry, and what triggers them.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
